reasoning: The question asks for the probability that the temperature on unspecified resolution dates (presumably the close date of 2026-01-24) will be higher than on 2025-10-26 at Tromelin Island. Tromelin is located in the Southern Hemisphere at latitude ~15.9°S. In this tropical oceanic climate, late January represents mid-summer with average daily temperatures typically around 28.5°C, while late October represents spring with averages around 25.5°C - 26.0°C (as demonstrated by the 25.8°C reading on 2025-10-16). Because Tromelin is a small oceanic island, the day-to-day temperature variance is very small. The main factor that could cause a January day to be cooler than an October day is a significant tropical cyclone bringing heavy rain and cloud cover, which could temporarily depress the daily average. However, given the high sea surface temperatures in January (~28°C), even rainy days rarely drop below 26°C on average. Therefore, the probability that the mid-summer temperature will be higher than the mid-spring temperature is very high.
